subject: First Year Topology
instructions: Work the following problems and show all work. Support all statements to the best of your ability. Answer problems 6–10 with complete definitions or statements or short proofs.

problems.text: Show that the set of functions \(f:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{Z}\) that are eventually zero is countable. A function \(f:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{Z}\) is called eventually zero if there is \(N\) such that \(f(n)=0\) for all \(n\ge N\).
Let \(X\) be a connected metric space having more than one point. Can \(X\) be countable?

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that every continuous map \(f:[0,1]\to[0,1]\) has a fixed point. Is this true for continuous maps

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Does there exist a continuous surjective map from the 2-sphere \(S^2\) to the interval \((0,1)\)?

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Does there exist a continuous injective map from the 2-sphere \(S^2\) to the interval \((0,1)\)?

problems.text: Let \(f:X\to X\) be a map of a compact metric space to itself that satisfies the following condition: \(d(f(x),f(y))<d(x,y)\) for all \(x\ne y\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that \(f\) has a fixed point and the fixed point is unique.

problems.text: Is every connected space path connected?
What is a basis of a topology? Does the set of all half-open intervals \(\{(a,b],[c,d)\mid a<b,\ c<d\}\) form a basis of a topology on \(\mathbb{R}\)?
Is \(\mathbb{R}^{\omega}\) connected in the uniform topology?
